[
  {
    "vr_id": 818,
    "reference": "Mat 13:21",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Thlipsis here names the inner pressure of persecution that tests and exposes the rootless person's inner response, causing them to fall away — inner-being impact of threat clearly in view.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 819,
    "reference": "Mat 24:9",
    "classification": "relevant",

    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {

    "reference": "Mar 4:17",

    "rationale": "Parallel to Mat 13:21; thlipsis names the pressure of persecution that tests the inner person's rootedness and causes immediate falling away — inner-being engagement clear.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 825,
    "reference": "Joh 16:21",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Thlipsis describes the anguish of a woman in labour as an inner-being experience of distress and pain, used by Jesus as analogy for the disciples' coming inner anguish and subsequent joy.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 827,
    "reference": "Act 7:10",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "God rescuing Joseph 'out of all his afflictions' implies the inner-being experience of distress and pressure Joseph endured; the rescue from thlipsis is the point of the statement.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 828,
    "reference": "Act 7:11",
    "classification": "borderline",
    "rationale": "Great affliction during famine could refer primarily to external hardship and suffering rather than inner-being anguish; the focus is on material deprivation with inner engagement only implied.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 829,
    "reference": "Act 11:19",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Thlipsis names the persecution that arose over Stephen, which scattered believers — the inner pressure of threat and persecution driving the community is the operative sense.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 831,
    "reference": "Act 20:23",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "The Holy Spirit warns Paul that afflictions await him in every city — thlipsis here names anticipated inner-being distress and external pressure that Paul braces himself to endure.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 832,
    "reference": "Rom 2:9",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Tribulation and distress are paired as the divine consequence bearing down on evildoers, naming the inner crushing anguish of judgment — squarely within the thlipsis M01 register.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 834,
    "reference": "Rom 8:35",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Thlipsis heads a list of circumstances that might threaten the believer's inner confidence in Christ's love — the inner-being question of whether pressure and anguish can separate is central.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 835,
    "reference": "Rom 12:12",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Be patient in tribulation is a direct inner-being exhortation — thlipsis names the pressure believers must inwardly endure with steadfastness, engaging the structural opposite of inner collapse.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 57969,
    "reference": "1Cor 7:28",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Worldly troubles (thlipsis) in marriage names the inner-being pressures and distress of married life that Paul wishes to spare believers — inner burden and anguish of circumstance in view.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 57972,
    "reference": "2Cor 2:4",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Paul explicitly locates thlipsis as the inner-being source of his tearful letter — 'much affliction and anguish of heart' names the constitutional inner experience of distress driving his writing.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 57975,
    "reference": "2Cor 7:4",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "In all our affliction Paul overflows with joy — thlipsis names the inner-being pressure he endures, and the structural opposite (joy overcoming affliction) makes M01 inner-being content explicit.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 57976,
    "reference": "2Cor 8:13",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Thlipsis describes the inner burden of being pressed/weighed down that Paul does not wish to impose on the Corinthians — inner-being sense of being burdened under pressure is in view.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 836,
    "reference": "Eph 3:13",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Paul's suffering (thlipsis) for the Ephesians and his exhortation not to lose heart over it engages the inner-being impact of his affliction on both himself and his readers.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 844,
    "reference": "2Th 1:6",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "God repaying affliction to those who afflict — thlipsis names the crushing divine judgment bearing down on persecutors, the inner-being weight of retributive distress clearly in view.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  },
  {
    "vr_id": 846,
    "reference": "Jam 1:27",
    "classification": "relevant",
    "rationale": "Visiting orphans and widows in their affliction targets people whose inner-being experience of distress and anguish under loss and vulnerability is the reason for care — M01 register present.",
    "set_aside_reason": ""
  }
]
